title: Validate final acceptance criteria and release readiness for v0.4.6
summary: Confirm that the v0.4.6 implementation resolves Zed multi-project startup failures caused by CWD='/', enforces one-time workspace root initialization through `workspace_initialize(cwd)`, preserves safety against `/.agent/` path expansion, persists failed tool executions to `.agent/errors.jsonl`, and satisfies the release coverage target.
acceptance_criteria: The MCP server can start under Zed multi-project scenarios even when the process working directory is '/'.
The server supports a one-time `workspace_initialize(cwd)` flow that binds the canonical project root for the session.
`workspace_initialize(cwd)` rejects '/' and rejects rebinding to a different project root, while allowing same-root reinitialization as a no-op success if implemented that way in the final design.
Root-dependent tool calls do not read from or write to `/.agent/`.
Root-dependent tool calls fail clearly before initialization when startup cwd is unresolved.
Failed tool executions are appended to `.agent/errors.jsonl` with tool name, input, and output/error details.
`.rules` and the `zed-agentops-init.sh` scaffolded heredoc both reflect the new startup contract requiring workspace initialization before canonical state restore.
Verification passes and reported test coverage remains at or above 90%.

steps.description: Review the implemented startup flow against the v0.4.6 plan, confirming unresolved '/' startup is tolerated until `workspace_initialize(cwd)` binds the project root.
Confirm that repo/artifact operations remain protected against `/.agent/` resolution and that uninitialized root-dependent calls fail with explicit guidance.
Check that failed tool execution logging to `.agent/errors.jsonl` is implemented with the required fields and does not suppress the original client-facing error response.
Verify that `.rules`, the scaffolded `.rules` heredoc in `zed-agentops-init.sh`, and release-facing documentation are aligned with the final startup and error-logging contract.
Confirm that verification results and coverage output satisfy the release threshold and that no acceptance criterion from the plan or draft has been omitted.

notes: Focus on final release acceptance, not on introducing additional implementation changes.
Acceptance review must cover both the workspace initialization contract and the new persistent tool error logging behavior.
Keep the final validation evidence concise and tied directly to the documented acceptance criteria.
README.md and README-jp.md remain unchanged for this ticket because they are general-user documentation rather than release-operator workflow documentation.

startup_behavior.summary: The MCP server tolerates startup with CWD='/' in unresolved mode and binds the project root through workspace_initialize(cwd).

root_safety.summary: Root-dependent operations remain protected against /.agent/ resolution and fail clearly before initialization when the repo root is unresolved.

error_logging.summary: Failed tool executions are recorded in .agent/errors.jsonl with tool context while preserving the original client-facing error.

rules_alignment.summary: .rules and the scaffolded heredoc in zed-agentops-init.sh both require workspace_initialize(cwd) before canonical restore.

verification.summary: pytest -q passed with 289 tests, repo_verify passed, and release coverage reached 95%.

documentation.summary: README changes were intentionally skipped because the README files are scoped to general users, not this release-readiness ticket.
